FULL CONTENT:
![Machine Design](https://img.machinedesign.com/files/base/ebm/machinedesign/image/static/machinedesign-logo.png?auto=format,compress&fit=fill&fill=solid&height=35)
![Engineer looking at display](https://img.machinedesign.com/files/base/ebm/machinedesign/image/2022/10/ame02.6349975bed22e.png?auto=format,compress&fit=crop&q=45&h=139&height=139&w=250&width=250 "Engineer looking at display")

# Advanced Machine Engineering: Technology Changes are Driving Transformation

**At a Glance:**

The industrial machinery landscape is rife with unprecedented challenges—supply chain shortages, labor scarcity and entrepreneurial market disruptions. At the same time, machine builders are tackling outdated legacy processes and technologies that threaten growth. These combined industry megatrends are pressing machine builders to rethink how they respond to fluctuating market demands while optimizing quality to remain competitive.

At the same time, changing consumer preferences are driving product manufacturers’ need for highly agile, flexible machines capable of creating a wide range of products. Smarter machines are required that integrate hardware, software and services into one cohesive environment. Machine builders need greater automation to drive higher efficiencies into their processes while competitively differentiating themselves. Moreover, automation can increase sustainability within their operations while promoting the sustainability goals of their end customers.

All these trends are motivating machine builders to transform their day-to-day practices digitally. By implementing a digital thread approach to engineering, machine builders can rapidly deliver tomorrow’s highly complex machines, gaining a competitive advantage against those unencumbered by legacy processes. The digital thread approach takes the form of Advanced Machine Engineering to drive greater certainty in creating next-generation machines. It leverages a cloud-based methodology that encourages collaboration among the fundamental engineering disciplines leading to a simulation-driven digital twin. The result is greater flexibility while improving quality and time-to-market.

***[READ MORE:](https://www.machinedesign.com/automation-iiot/article/21250762/imts-main-stage-glocalization-can-bring-home-manufacturing) [IMTS Main Stage: “Glocalization” Can Bring Home Manufacturing](https://www.machinedesign.com/automation-iiot/article/21250762/imts-main-stage-glocalization-can-bring-home-manufacturing)***

![Advanced machine engineering leverages the easy accessibility of the cloud to create visibility across the many teams needed to develop today's smart machines.](https://img.machinedesign.com/files/base/ebm/machinedesign/image/2022/10/ame01.6349975b10845.png?auto=format,compress&fit=max&q=45&w=250&width=250 "Advanced machine engineering leverages the easy accessibility of the cloud to create visibility across the many teams needed to develop today's smart machines.")

### Cloud-based, Multi-discipline Collaboration

Advanced machine engineering leverages the easy accessibility of the cloud to create visibility across the many teams needed to develop today’s smart machines. It encompasses all the engineering disciplines—mechanical, electrical, electronics and software engineering—and merges them into a single source of truth via the cloud. In addition, the approach maximizes data reuse and supports an integrated change process to ensure a standard delivery process.

Engineering teams can now store, access and utilize real-time dashboards, schedules and documents at any time, from anywhere. It drives efficiency into the machine development process, boosting overall productivity. In addition, machine builders can concurrently capture and incorporate design feedback from numerous internal and external stakeholders. This ensures the creation of the most effective machines possible while avoiding delays and managing product costs. By incorporating feedback earlier into the design process, machine builders can also account for supply chain issues and source materials from outside vendors.

***[READ MORE: Digital Transformation: The CEO of the Future is a Technologist, Not an MBA](https://www.machinedesign.com/automation-iiot/article/21240196/intellic-integration-digital-transformation-the-ceo-of-the-future-is-a-technologist-not-an-mba)***

![Advanced machine engineering is an approach that leverages a cloud-based methodology to encourage collaboration among engineering disciplines. One payoff is a simulation-driven digital twin.](https://img.machinedesign.com/files/base/ebm/machinedesign/image/2022/10/ame02_resized.6349975b1df5a.png?auto=format,compress&fit=max&q=45&w=250&width=250 "Advanced machine engineering is an approach that leverages a cloud-based methodology to encourage collaboration among engineering disciplines. One payoff is a simulation-driven digital twin.")

### Simulation-driven Digital Twin & Virtual Commissioning

Cloud-based technology is a critical enabler for creating the digital twin in advanced machine engineering. By streamlining communication between disciplines, the cloud encourages the sharing of data vital to creating a comprehensive digital twin of the future machine. The multi-disciplinary model of the proposed machine connects mechanical, electrical and automation engineers to evaluate performance, energy use, output and other key indicators. Then a digital twin can simulate everything virtually, reducing the need for costly and time-consuming physical prototyping. The virtual process, based on simulation and the digital twin, offers benefits across the entire development and deployment of an advanced machine:

***[READ MORE: The IIoT Journey Starts With a Good Partnership](https://www.machinedesign.com/automation-iiot/article/21250515/emerson-the-iiot-journey-starts-with-a-good-partnership)***

![In an advanced machine engineering environment, cloud-based technology act as the critical enabler for creating the digital twin.](https://img.machinedesign.com/files/base/ebm/machinedesign/image/2022/10/ame06_resized.6349975b6de77.png?auto=format,compress&fit=max&q=45&w=250&width=250 "In an advanced machine engineering environment, cloud-based technology act as the critical enabler for creating the digital twin.")

### Modular Design Leads to Mass Customization

Advanced machine engineering provides the foundation for true modular design with cloud collaboration and the digital twin. This setup is a highly desirable approach where machine information and specifications are contained within modular “blocks.” These blocks can be reused across projects and customized to fit customer expectations. For example, engineers can open a block that contains a preexisting machine’s bill of material. They then can re-save the block as a new project and alter it to include updated parts or components that meet a customer’s specifications.

Using modular design, machine builders have a cost-effective path to scale for mass customization. It is now possible to produce highly differentiated machines quickly and cost-effectively from a shared set of modules.

As a result, machine builders can accelerate their bid times and respond to a larger number of inquiries and deliver a larger number of orders, expanding their business’s profitability. Modular design is also vital for rapid machine configuration. When customer requirements change, machine builders have an easily accessible process for updating machine programming logic or refreshing machine models. It is also possible to trace design iterations and understand why specific decisions must be made. The digital twin of machinery makes all this possible by offering a virtual mockup of a machine, where machine data can readily be referenced and updated.

### Delivering Complex Machines Successfully

Advanced machine engineering gives machine builders the powerful capabilities needed to survive and thrive in today’s demanding industrial landscape. Integrating all the engineering disciplines empowers collaboration across the design teams through real-time cloud tools.

The result is the comprehensive digital twin of the machine that supports sophisticated simulation to create optimized machines without the need for physical prototyping. In addition, this method creates an environment for true modular design, where machine builders can quickly and cost-effectively adapt to shifting market requirements. Altogether, advanced machine engineering enhances machine builders’ productivity, speed-to-market and quality, helping them win big in the market every time.

*Rahul Garg is the vice president for Industrial Machinery & SMB Programs at Siemens, responsible for defining and delivering key strategic initiatives and solutions and global business development.*

### Rahul Garg | Vice President for Industrial Machinery & SMB Programs

Rahul Garg is the vice president for Industrial Machinery & SMB Programs at Siemens Digital Industries Software, responsible for defining and delivering key strategic initiatives and solutions and global business development. He and his team are responsible for identifying key initiatives and developing solutions for the industry while working closely with industry leading customers and providing thought leadership on new and emerging issues faced by the machinery industry.

Garg’s experience and insight are derived from a 25-year career delivering software-based solutions for product engineering and manufacturing innovation for the global manufacturing industry, spanning a career in R&D to program management, sales and P&L management and having focused exclusively on the industrial machinery and heavy equipment industry since 2007.

# 6 Machine Design Engineering Challenges: Part 1

![6 Machine Design Engineering Challenges: Part 1](https://technosofteng.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/June-Blog-2.webp "6 Machine Design Engineering Challenges: Part 1")

## Key takeaways

In this article, we will cover reliability and flexibility as machine design engineering challenges. You will also know how Technosoft Engineering can help overcome these challenges.

## Introduction

Whenever machine designers are busy designing a machine, various challenges may arise with the need to balance performance, safety, durability, and cost. But technological advancements can enhance the severity of such challenges even when client needs have to be met. Additionally, it may become tough to integrate new technologies into the machine design. Hence, designers have to innovate and optimize continuously to develop machines that are reliable and efficient at the same time. 

![Machine Design Engineering Challenge One: Reliability](https://technosofteng.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/June-Blog-2_Such-as-Robotic-Arm-in-Automotive-industry.jpg)

![Machine Design Engineering Challenge One: Reliability](https://technosofteng.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/June-Blog-2_Such-as-Robotic-Arm-in-Automotive-industry.jpg)

## **Machine Design Engineering Challenge One: Reliability**

Reliability claims can certainly indicate that the machine is working smoothly. But nowadays, companies use Overall Equipment Effectiveness. This is a metric that teams use to assess the overall efficiency and effectiveness of the machine. It also offers a way to determine how the manufacturing operations are performing compared to their full potential. When the machine is 100% effective, it simply means that it produces perfect products swiftly and without any downtime. 

As companies measure OEE and losses, it can help optimize manufacturing operations. It can also measure the return on investment once the machinery has been installed at a certain location. Through the data, companies can improve machine designs for the future. By setting up machines that have a high OEE score, it can be useful when organizations are looking forward to growth with repeat orders. Hence, when choosing a machine, companies must ensure that it delivers high-quality products in a short time span. However, quality engineering needs time; otherwise, it compromises machine design. Here’s when Technosoft Engineering can help improve reliability.

## **How Does Technosoft Expertise Help Improve Reliability?**

When your company starts working closely with Technosoft Engineering on a machine design, the team can ensure reliability through Finite Element Analysis (FEA). This technique can help optimize the durability of the design. Besides, the team will test the design in different environments to make sure that it meets stringent quality standards. Simulations and modeling tools further help to identify potential issues and optimize designs before engineers build prototypes. Through the integration of robust design principles, Technosoft ensures that machines are developed to withstand various environmental factors. Ultimately, a collaborative approach at Technosoft ensures that every aspect of machine reliability is taken into consideration, right from the initial design to the final implementation. 

![Machine Design Engineering Challenge Two: Flexibility](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%200%200'%3E%3C/svg%3E)![Machine Design Engineering Challenge Two: Flexibility](https://technosofteng.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/June-Blog-2_Automobile-Modular-platform.jpg)

![Machine Design Engineering Challenge Two: Flexibility](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%200%200'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
![Machine Design Engineering Challenge Two: Flexibility](https://technosofteng.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/June-Blog-2_Automobile-Modular-platform.jpg)

## **Machine Design Engineering Challenge Two: Flexibility**

Reducing the time for machine design is certainly advantageous around the world. When less time is required to design a machine, it leads to faster delivery and high margins. Manufacturers will also gain more flexibility when the development of multi-purpose machines reduces design time. But many manufacturers need more production lines to manufacture different products. They also need to upgrade the machines when there’s a need to manufacture new products.  

Manufacturers may consider a push button option to swap the products along the production line. But this operation will entirely depend on the similarities in the products and working with minimum downtime. The team will also think about various design strategies along with presenting a modular design. These improvements will, undoubtedly, make machines more flexible. It will also lead to making the machine and the design process more complex than they were in the past. In such a case, relevant data has to be maintained separately while trying every concept. Moving ahead, many problems will arise when machine designers are working on the data. 

## **How Does Technosoft’s Customer Centric Focus Help in Flexibility?**

Technosoft’s customer-centric focus certainly plays a vital role in enhancing the flexibility of its machine designs. Once the team understands your needs, the company excels at offering customized solutions as per your unique specifications. An agile design approach further helps to make adjustments across the design and development stages. Technosoft also leverages advanced tools and technologies to enhance machine design flexibility. While the team experiments with the design configurations, it can assess their flexibility. This helps to meet the evolving needs of the clients. The team is also proactive in solving problems to make sure that issues are identified and addressed at an early stage of the design process.

## **Partner with Technosoft Engineering**

As Technosoft Engineering takes machine design inspirations to the next level, a skillful and dedicated team helps enhance durability and performance. Optimizing expenses and manufacturing processes also helps to ensure the cost-effective development of machines. Eventually, you will be leveraging a 20-year legacy of workmanship while the team assists, right from conceptualization to structural design. The team also helps with CAD modeling and simulation for developing the machine precisely. 

## **Closing Thoughts**

Finally, it’s always important to ensure reliability and flexibility in machine design. Reliability ensures that the machine performs consistently and safely with less downtime. This not only helps in maintaining productivity but also safety standards. Flexibility, on the contrary, enables the team to adapt to changing operational requirements and technological advancements. The team can accommodate new processes and market demands without the need for redesigns. 

If you’re thinking about developing a unique machine right from scratch, [contact](/contact-us) Technosoft Engineering today.

In the next article, we will speak about Speed and Precision, and Modular Design Flexibility.

# [Myer-Hall Machine Works](https://myer-hall.com)

# Top Challenges in Custom Machinery Design & How to Overcome Them

###### SHARE

![Share on LinkedIn](https://myer-hall.com/wp-content/themes/alfresco/pc-elements/images/icon-linkedin.png)
![Share on Facebook](https://myer-hall.com/wp-content/themes/alfresco/pc-elements/images/icon-facebook.png)

*November 18, 2024*

Designing custom machinery isn’t just about fixing a problem – it’s about precision, performance, and making sure your equipment does exactly what it needs to, every single time. Manufacturers with unique demands can’t rely on off-the-shelf solutions; they need custom machinery that delivers on specific production goals. But let’s be clear: building this kind of equipment is a serious undertaking. Complexities pile up fast, and if you’re not prepared, unexpected expenses will catch you off guard.

The key to success? Understanding the challenges before they hit. Here’s what you need to watch for – obstacles that can derail your project and eat into your budget if left unchecked. Keep these in mind as you plan for your next custom machine, and you’ll be a step ahead, not scrambling to keep up.

## **Do-It-Yourself (DIY) Machinery Planning often sets you back**

One of the biggest misconceptions I see is companies thinking they can handle custom machinery design in-house. I get it – DIY sounds like the cost-effective route. But here’s the reality: many who start down this road quickly realize they lack the specific expertise and resources to get the job done, and by then, the costs start piling up fast.

Let’s break down why: First, when you take on designing machinery, you’re diverting energy and attention away from what keeps your business moving – getting product out the door for your customers. That can mean delays for your core business or for the machine itself, and neither is good for your bottom line. Second, engineering custom machinery isn’t as straightforward as it seems. There are nuances that are costly to overlook: things like space limitations, power requirements, ventilation, safety standards, and programming that fits the skills of your existing team. When these details get missed early on, they’re exponentially more expensive to fix later.

In my experience, DIY attempts often lead to stalled or abandoned projects – or worse, machines that simply don’t perform as intended. We frequently step in when companies realize their design can’t deliver the results they need. At that point, it’s no longer just a design job; it’s about correcting costly missteps, rebuilding, and getting it right.

### The takeaway: A DIY approach is deceptively expensive and often requires additional expert fixes, costing even more time and money. Save yourself the wasted time and money by getting the right expertise from the start.

## **Design Complications Are Not Just About Resourcing and Budgets**

Many custom machinery projects start with a vague concept – clients have a general idea of what they want but lack a precise vision. That’s a recipe for trouble. Designing effective machinery requires clear, specific goals from day one. Before diving into the build, it’s crucial to understand exactly what the machine needs to do, the quality standards it must meet, and the outcomes it has to deliver. Without this clarity, even the best-laid plans can go off the rails.

Going beyond the concept phase means investing time in detailed planning and using 3D modeling to ensure precision. Don’t just design; simulate. With 3D modeling and virtual testing, you can see the machine in action before any physical build begins. This approach prevents costly rework and ensures the final product meets every demand.

If you’re aiming for a successful build, hyper-planning is your friend. Think through every detail of what the equipment needs to do – including potential variations that might come up in daily operations. Solid planning from the start minimizes costly surprises and keeps delays at bay. Imagine this: a company wants a machine for quality control but only plans for front and back cameras, missing the need for 360-degree verification. That oversight can easily lead to expensive redesigns.

Other critical considerations for the design and planning phase:

### The takeaway: Start your custom machinery project by covering every angle in planning, and you’ll save time, money, and effort down the road.

## **Planning Your Project Management**

Building custom manufacturing equipment has its own set of hidden challenges. One of the biggest is budgeting. Budgets are a hard reality check; no amount of creative thinking can compensate for the real need for adequate parts and resources. Establishing a clear budget isn’t just about securing funding—it sets boundaries and helps manage expectations for the final result, especially among key stakeholders and operators who’ll work with the finished machine. Using a structured approach like Front-End Engineering Level (FEL) planning is invaluable here. It enables better budget management and planning before the big costs start rolling in.

Another common challenge is unexpected delivery delays. Many critical parts are manufactured overseas, so disruptions in the supply chain can stall a project. While you can’t always predict when these delays will hit, the best approach is to start your build well before any critical equipment hits its end of life. This buffer helps keep you operational without being at the mercy of supply chain uncertainties. Recently, for instance, lead times jumped from a few weeks to nearly six months. With such delays, proactive project management becomes essential to prevent extended downtime.

Market demand shifts can also threaten the relevance of a custom machine even before it’s fully operational. While large corporations tend to be more reactive to these changes, smaller manufacturers often have an edge—they stay focused on progress rather than adapting to every economic fluctuation. This agility gives small and mid-sized companies a competitive advantage. Collaborating with custom machinery engineers can provide the flexibility to pivot when demands shift, helping you stay on track regardless of market volatility.

### Key takeaway: Set clear budgeting expectations, get a head start on the build before things reach a crisis point, and stay steady through market disruptions. This approach positions you to handle obstacles with minimal impact on production.

In the end, when you’re considering custom machinery, set yourself up for success by focusing on these fundamentals:

And always remember: the best time to act is now. It’s more cost-effective to move forward today than to wait for next year’s inevitable price increases. The cost of building manufacturing machinery rarely goes down, and delaying often means missed opportunities. Upgrading technology pays off—not only in productivity gains but also in boosting team morale, giving people a stronger sense of pride and positivity in their work.

With new technologies emerging, there is an increasing necessity for comprehensive training programs to equip personnel with the skills needed to effectively operate advanced machinery.
